department: Financeiro (Contas a Pagar + Contas a Receber)

ai_agent.role: Manages all financial flows: accounts payable (vendor/sub payments with PO verification, notarization, and Leo approval), accounts receivable (draw collection from clients), house closing accounting, QuickBooks reconciliation, field expense allocation, and financial reporting (DRE, balance sheets).
workflow_chains: invoice_received -> verify_po -> create_bill -> confirm_checklist_done -> send_release -> notarize -> leo_approve -> process_payment -> reconcile_quickbooks
contract_signed -> verify_draws_in_bt -> edit_draw_values -> send_collection -> track_payment -> apply_hold_if_delayed

staff.name: Fernanda Franco
staff.role: Financial Supervisor / Notary - executes payments at the bank, supervises financial operations, processes daily payment batches

staff.name: Iara Oliveira
staff.role: Accounts Payable - processes invoices, verifies POs, creates bills, manages releases, QuickBooks classification (86 bills = 2hrs daily), handles field expense allocation

staff.name: Rayff Cunha
staff.role: Accounts Payable - processes invoices, verifies POs, creates bills, manages releases and notarization, QuickBooks reconciliation, handles repair invoices without POs

staff.name: Cinthia Araujo
staff.role: Accounts Receivable - manages draw collection from clients, verifies draw values against contracts, processes partial payments manually, applies hold when payment delayed, pays permit fees on county portal

staff.name: Thabata Goncalves
staff.role: House Closing - manages financial close-out of completed houses, creates artificial bills when real invoices haven't arrived, ensures complete financial picture before closing

staff.name: Ebano Maia
staff.role: Financial team member - general financial operations support

staff.name: Rafaella Almeida
staff.role: Financial team member - general financial operations support

staff.name: Bruna Carvalho
staff.role: Utilities and Insurance - manages utility payment processing, insurance documentation, builders risk coordination

core_knowledge.description: Complete payment cycle: 1) Receive invoice by email (address, service, value), 2) Verify matching PO in BuilderTrend, 3) Create 'bill' in system, 4) Register bill in internal spreadsheet, 5) Confirm service was performed (checklist done from Scheduling), 6) Send 'release' to sub for notarization, 7) Sub gets release notarized at public notary, 8) Receive notarized release (photo/PDF), 9) Build daily payment spreadsheet, 10) Send to Fernanda for bank execution, 11) Leo approves payment, 12) Payment processed daily in afternoon, 13) Next day: close out in BuilderTrend and QuickBooks, 14) Add class in QuickBooks (CMER + house number), 15) Bank reconciliation in QB transactions tab.

Certain payment types do NOT require notarized release: initial permit fees, architect services, materials (Lisa products, doors, light fixtures), septic services, and tank services. All other sub payments require the sub to take the release document to a public notary for notarization before payment can be processed.

Draw schedule controls cash flow per house: Draw 1 (15-20% - site work through forms), Draw 2 (25-30% - Phase 1 block/roughs), Draw 3 (30-35% - Phase 2 drywall/paint/trim), Draw 4 (15-20% - final/landscaping/CO). Draw values in BuilderTrend may differ from contract when special client agreements exist. If draw payment delayed, Financeiro applies hold blocking Scheduling from advancing past that draw's scope.

Every bill must be classified in QuickBooks with a 'class' tag (format: CMER-### for project identification). Processing 86 bills takes approximately 2 hours due to two slow systems (BuilderTrend and QuickBooks) running simultaneously. This is the single most time-consuming daily task in the department.

When a house needs to close financially but real invoices haven't arrived, Thabata creates 'artificial bills' in BuilderTrend based on estimated average values from similar projects. This prevents financial closing delays. When the real invoice arrives, it replaces the artificial one. Critical because after closing, Lakeshore can no longer charge the homeowner.

Field teams make emergency purchases with company credit cards. Receipts are sent via Slack channel. Financeiro must identify the material/service from often incomplete receipts, allocate cost to the correct house(s), and rateio (split) across multiple houses when applicable. Unidentifiable expenses are classified as 'variances'.

Supervisors frequently request repairs without creating POs in BuilderTrend. When repair invoices arrive without matching POs, the team must contact Moises or Samuel for value authorization (often slow). After authorization, a retroactive PO is created and payment processed. Repairs with fixed values (e.g., Eco One) are more straightforward.

FOCO payments split 40-40-20 across three milestones; ECOWAN splits 40-30-30. Financeiro must verify that invoices linked to a PO do not exceed the PO value and that the correct split percentage is applied at each milestone. Values may change with volume, and contracts are not always updated to reflect current pricing.

daily_workflows.name: Accounts Payable - Daily Invoice Processing
steps: Receive invoices by email (high daily volume)
For each invoice: verify matching PO exists in BuilderTrend
If PO exists and value matches: create bill in BuilderTrend
Register bill in internal control spreadsheet
Verify checklist done from Scheduling (service confirmed)
Send release document to sub for notarization (unless exempt category)
Receive notarized release (photo/PDF by email)
Add to daily payment spreadsheet
Submit payment spreadsheet to Fernanda for bank processing
Leo approves payment batch
Fernanda executes payments at the bank (afternoon processing)
Next day: close bill in BuilderTrend and QuickBooks
Add QuickBooks class: CMER + house number
Reconcile in QuickBooks transactions tab
critical_decisions: Whether invoice matches PO value within acceptable tolerance
Whether service has been confirmed (checklist done)

daily_workflows.name: Repair Invoice Processing (No PO)
steps: Receive repair invoice without matching PO in BuilderTrend
Identify that supervisor requested repair without creating PO
Contact Moises or Samuel via Slack/WhatsApp for value authorization
Wait for authorization response (can be slow)
Upon authorization: create retroactive PO in BuilderTrend
Process payment through standard flow
critical_decisions: Whether to escalate if authorization is delayed beyond threshold
Whether repair value is reasonable based on historical data

daily_workflows.name: Accounts Receivable - Draw Management
steps: Receive notification of contract signing from Permits (Cacia) via Slack
Verify draw schedule in BuilderTrend matches contract values
Manually edit draw values when client agreements differ from standard
Send draw collection to client via email at construction milestone triggers
Monitor payment receipt
If payment delayed: apply manual hold in BuilderTrend blocking construction advancement
For partial payments: track manually in spreadsheet (system doesn't support)
critical_decisions: When to apply hold for delayed payment
Whether to accept partial payment (manual tracking required)
Draw value adjustments for special client agreements

daily_workflows.name: Field Expense Processing
steps: Monitor Slack channel for field purchase receipts
Identify material/service from receipt (often incomplete information)
Determine which house(s) the expense applies to
Rateio (split) across multiple houses when applicable
Register in appropriate system with house allocation
Classify as 'variance' when material/house cannot be determined
critical_decisions: House allocation when receipt information is insufficient
Whether to classify as variance or request clarification from field

daily_workflows.name: House Financial Closing
steps: Identify houses ready for financial close-out
Verify all real invoices have been received and processed
For missing invoices: create artificial bills based on estimated values
Record artificial bills for future replacement when real invoices arrive
Complete final bill reconciliation in BuilderTrend
Close accounting in QuickBooks with complete financial picture
Tag daily log: #F$ - ACCOUNTING CLOSED
critical_decisions: Whether to close with artificial bills or wait for real invoices
Estimated values for artificial bills (accuracy critical - cannot charge owner after closing)

features_used: Budget vs projected vs actual cost comparison per project
Bills tracking from all sources (POs, Service Orders, Permitting Fees)
Draw/payment tracking with Schedule of Value integration
Exceeding costs management with approval workflow
api_endpoints: /finances/budget/{project_id} - GET budget comparison (estimated vs projected vs actual)
/finances/payments/draws/{project_id} - GET draw status and payment progress

features_used: Verified POs review with status pipeline (Pending Invoices, Ready for Payment, Processing, Authorized, Paid)
Payment batch creation for bulk processing
Subcontractor payment with period filtering and batch marking
api_endpoints: /purchase-orders - GET/PUT purchase order management with status tracking

features_used: Contract payment tracking (draw progress per contract)
Exceeding costs tracking for utility services (read-only budget threshold events)

handoffs.trigger: Checklist Done - supervisor confirms service was performed in BuilderTrend
handoffs.data_exchanged: Checklist completion confirmation with service details, house number, sub identification
handoffs.impact: PAYMENT TRIGGER - without checklist done, payment cannot be released to sub. This is the primary gate for accounts payable.

handoffs.trigger: Draw Released - client draw payment received and processed
handoffs.data_exchanged: Draw payment confirmation, amount received, remaining balance
handoffs.impact: Scheduling can only advance construction past a draw's scope after payment is confirmed. Delayed draws trigger holds.

handoffs.trigger: Contract Signed - notification from Permits (Cacia) that client signed construction contract
handoffs.data_exchanged: Signed contract, draw schedule, contract value, client information
handoffs.impact: Initiates draw schedule setup in BuilderTrend and first draw collection

handoffs.data_exchanged: PO reference values, budget totals by cost code, MEP payment split schedules
handoffs.impact: Financeiro uses budget values as reference to verify invoice amounts against POs

handoffs.data_exchanged: Purchase records, material allocation per house, rateio documentation
handoffs.impact: Purchases replicated in BuilderTrend feed into bill creation and QuickBooks classification

handoffs.trigger: Permit Ready to Issue (inbound) / Permit Payment Confirmed (outbound)
handoffs.data_exchanged: Permit payment request with county portal link (inbound), payment confirmation (outbound)
handoffs.impact: Permit cannot be issued without Financeiro making the county portal payment. Builders Risk filing blocked until payment confirmed.

handoffs.data_exchanged: Utility account details, amounts due, house allocation
handoffs.impact: Utility payments must be processed to maintain services during construction

handoffs.trigger: Emergency purchases made with company credit cards
handoffs.data_exchanged: Purchase receipts via Slack channel, material/service description (often incomplete)
handoffs.impact: Must be allocated to correct house for accurate per-project cost tracking

handoffs.trigger: Payment batch ready for approval / Repair authorization needed
handoffs.data_exchanged: Daily payment spreadsheet for Leo's approval, repair value authorization requests
handoffs.impact: No payment processed without Leo's approval. Repair authorization delays block vendor payment.

pain_points.description: Manual QuickBooks classification of 86 bills takes approximately 2 hours daily. Two slow systems (BuilderTrend and QuickBooks) must be used simultaneously. Classified as 'super demorado' (extremely slow) by the team.
pain_points.current_workaround: Iara/Rayff dedicate 2+ hours daily to manual classification; no shortcut possible with current tools
pain_points.proposed_solution: Automated integration between construction management system and QuickBooks with automatic class mapping (CMER-### per house)

pain_points.description: Invoices arrive without matching POs. Supervisors request repairs and services without creating POs in BuilderTrend first. This creates a repetitive workflow of tracking down authorization from Moises or Samuel.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual outreach to Moises/Samuel via Slack/WhatsApp; retroactive PO creation after authorization
pain_points.proposed_solution: Mobile PO creation requirement before service execution; supervisors must create PO via app with photo evidence before authorizing any work

pain_points.description: Missing contracts with exact values for 27+ models. Prices change based on volume and negotiation; the finance team often doesn't know the correct current contract value for each vendor/model combination.
pain_points.current_workaround: Team checks with Budget team or asks Samuel/Moises when invoice value doesn't match expected amount
pain_points.proposed_solution: Centralized contract database with current pricing, volume tiers, and automatic alerts when contracts are outdated

pain_points.description: Delayed repair confirmation. Authorization from Moises or Samuel for repair invoices without POs is frequently delayed, blocking vendor payment and creating sub dissatisfaction.
pain_points.current_workaround: Repeated follow-up via Slack and WhatsApp; payment delayed until response received
pain_points.proposed_solution: Automated approval workflow with escalation timer: if not approved within 48 hours, auto-escalate to next authority level

pain_points.description: No partial draw payment support in the current system. Clients sometimes make partial draw payments, but BuilderTrend cannot record partial payments, requiring manual spreadsheet tracking.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual tracking in Excel spreadsheets; risk of reconciliation errors
pain_points.proposed_solution: Native partial payment support in the construction management system with automatic balance calculation and status updates

pain_points.description: BuilderTrend-QuickBooks integration is incomplete. Date of maturity doesn't work as filter criteria; many fields don't map correctly between systems.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual data transfer between systems; double-entry of many fields
pain_points.proposed_solution: Direct bi-directional integration with field mapping between construction system and QuickBooks

pain_points.description: Field expenses arrive with incomplete information. Receipts sent via Slack often lack project/house identification, making accurate cost allocation impossible. Team resorts to 'guessing' (achismos).
pain_points.current_workaround: Best-guess allocation; unidentifiable expenses classified as variances
pain_points.proposed_solution: Mobile receipt submission app with mandatory fields: house number, cost code category, photo of receipt, description. Reject submissions with missing fields.

pain_points.description: BuilderTrend notifications to Permits team don't work. Permits cannot effectively notify Financeiro of payment needs through the system.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual notification via email and Slack between Permits and Financeiro
pain_points.proposed_solution: Reliable notification system with dedicated channels for payment requests and confirmations

pain_points.description: No complete financial history view for a house. When closing a house, there's no single view showing all financial transactions, making close-out slow and audit-prone.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual compilation of bills, payments, and draws from multiple sources
pain_points.proposed_solution: 360-degree house financial view aggregating all transactions: POs, bills, payments, draws, field expenses, variances

tribal_knowledge.description: Iara and Rayff know which payment categories are exempt from notarization requirements: initial permit fees, architect services, materials (Lisa products, doors, light fixtures), septic services, tank services. This knowledge determines whether a release document needs to be sent to the sub and delays payment processing if incorrectly classified.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: High - new staff would either require unnecessary notarization (delays) or skip required notarization (legal risk)

tribal_knowledge.description: When field receipts lack clear house identification, Iara and Rayff use heuristics based on: which houses are in active construction near the purchase location, which supervisors used the card, what materials were purchased and which construction phase they match, and historical patterns. This is entirely experience-based with no documented rules.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: High - field expenses would be disproportionately classified as variances, distorting per-house cost tracking

tribal_knowledge.description: Thabata knows how to estimate artificial bill values for house closing based on historical averages from similar completed houses. She considers: model type, county, vendor patterns, and seasonal price variations. Overestimation wastes budget; underestimation means Lakeshore absorbs the difference after closing.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: High - inaccurate artificial bills could lead to financial losses at house closing

tribal_knowledge.description: Cinthia knows which clients have special payment agreements that differ from standard draw schedules, which lenders require specific documentation before releasing draws, and historical patterns of payment delays by client type. She also knows when to apply holds vs when to continue construction on trust.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: Medium - could lead to construction advancing without payment security or unnecessary holds on reliable clients

tribal_knowledge.description: The CMER + house number classification system in QuickBooks and its relationship to BuilderTrend job numbers. Includes knowledge of which project codes map to which QuickBooks classes, how to handle multi-unit condominiums, and how to reconcile when the same vendor serves multiple houses.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: Medium - incorrect classification breaks per-project financial reporting and audit trail

topics: Complete payment cycle: invoice through QuickBooks reconciliation (15 steps)
Release document preparation and notarization requirements
Notarization exceptions (permits, architects, materials, septic, tank)
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Payment cycle steps, PO verification, notarization rules, approval requirements

topics: CMER class structure and project identification
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Class assignment rules, reconciliation process, discrepancy resolution

topics: Draw schedule structure: 4 draws with percentage splits
Permit fee payment on county portal
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Draw percentages, hold triggers, partial payment tracking, permit payment process

topics: Artificial bill creation and estimation methodology
When to close vs wait for real invoices
Final reconciliation in BuilderTrend and QuickBooks
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Closing prerequisites, artificial bill rules, post-closing implications

topics: Field receipt processing from Slack channel
House allocation methodology for unclear receipts
Repair invoice without PO: authorization workflow
training_curriculum.quiz_coverage: Expense allocation logic, variance triggers, repair authorization process

compliance_requirements.requirement: All sub payments require notarized release document except exempt categories
compliance_requirements.regulation: Florida Construction Lien Law - lien waiver requirement
compliance_requirements.verification: Notarized release on file for every sub payment; exempt categories documented per internal policy

compliance_requirements.requirement: All payments require Leo's explicit approval before bank execution
compliance_requirements.regulation: Internal financial control - dual authorization requirement
compliance_requirements.verification: Daily payment spreadsheet signed off by Leo before Fernanda processes at bank

compliance_requirements.requirement: Invoice must match PO value; deviations require authorization from Moises or Samuel
compliance_requirements.verification: Bill amount in BuilderTrend matches or is below PO amount; overages have documented authorization

compliance_requirements.requirement: QuickBooks class assigned to every bill for per-project financial tracking
compliance_requirements.regulation: Internal accounting standard - project-level cost tracking requirement
compliance_requirements.verification: Every bill in QuickBooks has CMER-### class; reconciliation confirms no unclassified bills

compliance_requirements.requirement: Builders Risk can only be filed after permit payment is confirmed
compliance_requirements.regulation: Insurance policy requirement - active permit prerequisite
compliance_requirements.verification: Permit payment confirmation on file before Builders Risk application is processed

compliance_requirements.requirement: Draw collections must follow contracted schedule; deviations require documented client agreement
compliance_requirements.verification: Draw values in BuilderTrend match contract; any manual adjustments have supporting documentation

key_metrics.target: All invoices received by noon processed same day
key_metrics.measurement: Count of invoices processed per day; backlog at end of day

key_metrics.target: < 1 hour for daily bill classification (currently 2 hours for 86 bills)
key_metrics.measurement: Time spent on QuickBooks class assignment per day

key_metrics.target: < 5% of invoices arrive without matching PO
key_metrics.measurement: Invoices without PO / total invoices received per month

key_metrics.target: < 24 hours from request to authorization from Moises/Samuel
key_metrics.measurement: Average hours from authorization request to response received

key_metrics.target: < 5 business days from milestone trigger to payment received
key_metrics.measurement: Days from construction milestone to draw payment deposit

key_metrics.target: < 3% artificial bill value deviation from actual
key_metrics.measurement: Average percentage difference between artificial bill estimate and real invoice when received

key_metrics.target: > 85% of field expenses correctly allocated to specific houses
key_metrics.measurement: Percentage of field expenses allocated to houses vs classified as variances

key_metrics.target: Invoice to payment in < 5 business days (after checklist done)
key_metrics.measurement: Days from invoice receipt to payment execution (when all gates are met)
